title: Dutch puff
description: This recipe is one of our favorite fancy breakfasts. It has many ingredients, but it’s not so difficult and long to cook. Dutch puff (also known as Dutch baby) together with café de olla and our [Good vibes playlist](https://music.apple.com/de/playlist/good-vibes-only/pl.u-2aoqXKzfG66mb65?l=en-GB) is the recipe for a perfect slow Sunday.
ingredients:
- 40 g all-purpose flour
- 40 g spelt flour
- 1 g ground cinnamon
- 1 g ground cardamom
- 2 g fine salt
- 4 eggs
- 180 g milk
- 20 g maple syrup
- 5 g vanilla extract
- 9 teaspoons butter
- 1 apple
- more maple syrup
  *optional, for serving*
- fresh berries
  *optional, for serving*
steps:
1. Preheat oven to 220C.
2. In a medium bowl, whisk together all dry ingredients: 40 g all-purpose flour, 40 g spelt flour, 1 g ground cinnamon, 1 g ground cardamom, and 2 g fine salt.
3. In a mixer bowl, beat together 4 eggs, 180 g milk, more maple syrup; optional, for serving, 5 g vanilla extract, on high speed with a whisk attachment for about 1 minute.
4. Add dry ingredients to the mixer bowl, and mix until all ingredients are combined.
5. Quarter the apple, remove the core, and thinly slice.
6. Melt 9 teaspoon butter in a medium-size cast iron skillet over medium heat. Add the apple, and cook occasionally stirring, until the butter starts to brown and apple starts to caramelize, for about 15 minutes.
7. Transfer the apples to a plate, but leave the butter in the skillet.
8. Pour the batter to the hot skillet, arrange half of the apples on top of the batter, stash the rest.
9. Put the skillet into the oven, and bake uncovered for about 15 minutes, until the Dutch puff is puffed up and browned on top. (Do not open the oven — it may deflate the Dutch puff.)
10. Serve topped with the remaining apples, a drizzle of maple syrup, and fresh berries.
tools:
- oven
- mixer
- medium-size cast iron skillet
source: This recipe is based on the “Double apple Dutch baby” recipe from “Half baked harvest every day” by Tieghan Gerard
